mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of a Decision Tree in Machine Learning?

options.text: Classification
Regression
Clustering
Dimensionality reduction

mcqs.explanation: Decision Trees are primarily used for classification tasks.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an advantage of Decision Trees?

options.text: Handling high-dimensional data
Handling categorical variables
Handling missing values
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: Decision Trees can handle high-dimensional data, categorical variables, and missing values.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of pruning in Decision Trees?

options.text: To reduce the complexity of the tree
To improve the accuracy of the tree
To handle missing values
To handle categorical variables

mcqs.explanation: Pruning is used to reduce the complexity of the tree and prevent overfitting.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a technique used to handle missing values in Decision Trees?

options.text: Surrogate splits
Ignoring missing values
Mean imputation
Median imputation

mcqs.explanation: Surrogate splits are used to handle missing values in Decision Trees.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a classification tree and a regression tree?

options.text: A classification tree is used for regression tasks, while a regression tree is used for classification tasks.
A classification tree is used for classification tasks, while a regression tree is used for regression tasks.
A classification tree is used for both classification and regression tasks.
A regression tree is used for both classification and regression tasks.

mcqs.explanation: A classification tree is used for classification tasks, while a regression tree is used for regression tasks.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a type of node in a Decision Tree?

options.text: Root node
Internal node
Leaf node
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: There are three types of nodes in a Decision Tree: root node, internal node, and leaf node.

mcqs.question: What is overfitting in Decision Trees?

options.text: When the tree is too simple and fails to capture the underlying pattern.
When the tree is too complex and fits the noise in the training data.
When the tree is too large and fails to handle missing values.
When the tree is too small and fails to handle categorical variables.

mcqs.explanation: Overfitting occurs when the tree is too complex and fits the noise in the training data rather than the underlying pattern.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a technique used to prevent overfitting in Decision Trees?

options.text: Pruning
Cross-validation
Reducing the tree size
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: Pruning, cross-validation, and reducing the tree size are all techniques used to prevent overfitting in Decision Trees.

mcqs.question: What is the importance of feature selection in Decision Trees?

options.text: Feature selection is not important in Decision Trees.
Feature selection is important in Decision Trees because it can improve the accuracy.
Feature selection is important in Decision Trees because it can reduce the complexity of the tree.
Feature selection is important in Decision Trees because it can improve the accuracy and reduce the complexity of the tree.

mcqs.explanation: Feature selection is important in Decision Trees because it can improve the accuracy and reduce the complexity of the tree.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a way to use Decision Trees for feature selection?

options.text: Analyzing the feature importance scores
Using a separate feature selection algorithm
Ignoring the feature selection step
Using a different machine learning algorithm

mcqs.explanation: Decision Trees can be used for feature selection by analyzing the feature importance scores.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a Decision Tree and a Random Forest?

options.text: A Decision Tree is a single tree, while a Random Forest is an ensemble of multiple trees.
A Decision Tree is an ensemble of multiple trees, while a Random Forest is a single tree.
A Decision Tree is used for classification tasks, while a Random Forest is used for regression tasks.
A Decision Tree is used for regression tasks, while a Random Forest is used for classification tasks.

mcqs.explanation: A Decision Tree is a single tree, while a Random Forest is an ensemble of multiple trees.

mcqs.question: Why are Decision Trees popular in Machine Learning?

options.text: Decision Trees are popular due to their simplicity.
Decision Trees are popular due to their interpretability.
Decision Trees are popular due to their ability to handle categorical and numerical data.
Decision Trees are popular due to all of the above.

mcqs.explanation: Decision Trees are popular due to their simplicity, interpretability, and ability to handle categorical and numerical data.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the root node in a Decision Tree?

options.text: The root node represents the entire dataset.
The root node represents a subset of the dataset.
The root node is used for feature selection.
The root node is used for pruning.

mcqs.explanation: The root node represents the entire dataset and is the starting point for the recursive partitioning process.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between an internal node and a leaf node in a Decision Tree?

options.text: An internal node is used for feature selection, while a leaf node is used for prediction.
An internal node is used for prediction, while a leaf node is used for feature selection.
An internal node is a node that has child nodes, while a leaf node is a node that does not have child nodes.
An internal node is a node that does not have child nodes, while a leaf node is a node that has child nodes.

mcqs.explanation: An internal node is a node that has child nodes, while a leaf node is a node that does not have child nodes.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the recursive partitioning process in a Decision Tree?

options.text: The recursive partitioning process is used to divide the dataset into smaller subsets.
The recursive partitioning process is used to combine the smaller subsets into a larger dataset.
The recursive partitioning process is used for feature selection.
The recursive partitioning process is used for pruning.

mcqs.explanation: The recursive partitioning process is used to divide the dataset into smaller subsets based on the input features.
